behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "request sent to the external API" shows a handoff boundary between request construction and API client dispatch. Ceiling-stop evidence: "includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels" is a payload-shape assertion; it does not require real HTTP behavior or model judgment quality. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text about network faults/OS/subprocess (System) and no text about evaluating model output correctness (Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to an in-process fake API client that captures outbound payload. Invoke classify() and assert captured request contains every taxonomy label, with no omissions.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "When the API returns a label" indicates boundary interaction between provider response parsing and taxonomy validation. Ceiling-stop evidence: "returns \"unknown\" instead of forwarding" is deterministic post-processing logic; no real network condition or model-quality judgment is required. Rejected higher levels: nothing in behavior requires live transport behavior (System) or semantic quality of model output (Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Run classify() and assert final returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets one local deterministic transformation: "normalization step always produces the same final label" for identical input. Failure is within normalization logic, not a module handoff or external dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert identical output label each run (table-driven with casing/whitespace variants of the same raw value).

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "repeated live API calls" and consistency tied to provider behavior. This depends on real model outputs and possible non-determinism, which mocks can hide. Rejected Workflow: this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same (document, taxonomy) under identical settings, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability whenever raw provider response is unchanged; flag drift events for investigation.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "before dispatch" requires coordination between budget enforcement and send path. Ceiling-stop evidence: "truncate/reject according to policy" is deterministic enforcement logic, not real network behavior and not model-quality dependent. Rejected higher levels: no requirement for real HTTP/OS effects (System) and no requirement to judge model output content (Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Configure low token budget and use a fake dispatcher. Verify over-budget input follows policy (truncate or reject) and that dispatched payload (if any) respects configured budget before send.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real provider accounting: "Provider-reported token usage" and execution through an external AI API. A stubbed response can always report compliant usage and hide real overages; actual model/runtime behavior affects reported usage. Rejected Workflow: single-call budget validation, not a multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls on representative documents, collect provider token-usage metadata per call, and assert each reported total is <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget report.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Unit-floor evidence: "API signals that the request rate is too high" plus "waits and retries" covers interaction between provider adapter, retry policy, and caller-facing result. Ceiling-stop evidence: "rather than returning an error to the caller" is control-flow/retry behavior; no requirement for real network timing faults or model decision quality. Rejected higher levels: no explicit need for live OS/network characteristics (System) and no dependence on model output semantics (Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API returning rate-limit error then success; use controllable clock/backoff hook. Assert module delays per policy, retries expected count, and finally returns successful label instead of surfacing error.
